concerns.text: FR-006 mandates 'CPU-only environment without requiring GPU acceleration' but provides no measurable threshold for 'feasibility' or 'time limit'. The Assumptions section mentions 'GitHub Actions free tier' but does not define the specific time budget (e.g., 6 hours) or memory limit (e.g., 7GB) against which this requirement is verified. Without a quantitative constraint, this requirement is unverifiable.

SC-005 states runtime is 'measured against the standard time limit of the GitHub Actions free-tier runner'. This is ambiguous and non-deterministic as 'standard time limit' varies by runner type (ubuntu-latest vs self-hosted) and job configuration. A verifiable SC must cite a specific numeric threshold (e.g., '≤ 3600 seconds') or a specific runner configuration file path.

FR-004 requires executing a 'repeated-measures ANOVA' but does not specify the verification procedure for the output. While US-3 mentions checking F-statistic and p-value, the FR itself lacks a deterministic check (e.g., 'System MUST output a JSON object containing keys: f_stat, p_value, eta_squared'). Without a defined output schema or validation script reference, the 'execution' of the analysis is not verifiable.

FR-007 requires validation of 'all required variables' but does not define the pass/fail criteria for this validation. Does it require 100% non-null values? A specific minimum count? Without a quantitative threshold (e.g., '≥ 95% non-null' or 'count ≥ 150'), the system cannot deterministically confirm if the requirement is met.
